channel.title: Blogs on KShut's Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on KShut's Portfolio

item.title: Project Reflect
item.description:
Posted on August 20, 2021

It took a while (mostly because of procrastination) but I’ve finally got a prototype of a simple bullet hell game where you can reflect (almost) everything that I call “Project Reflect” (working title).

Originally, I planned for a bullet hell game with a gimmick, so I based the game on this reflect mechanic. So far, it only reflects one type of bullet and pushes enemies, but I plan to add more interactions later.

Here’s what it looks like so far:
I wanted gameplay to be simple so the controls are also simple:

       WASD: Movement
 Left Click: Reflect (2 charges)
Right Click: Dash (not shown in gif)


When I tried deciding on a theme for the game, I somehow ended up with hololive (specifically Ayame) because that was what I was mostly interested in at the time. I admit I’m too far in the rabbithole to leave. With that said, the (rough) BGM is based on the songs Ayame have been a part of (or in other words, a Suspect and Hyakkaryouran Hanafubuki chiptune mashup):

There’s a lot I plan to change later in development, like replacing the health bar with “hearts”, updating the UI/font, adding a crosshair and other stuff. It’s also kinda boring right now with just one enemy and one bullet type, but I plan to add more enemies with different bullet patterns and maybe even lasers.

Development may be even slower now that summer break is over, but I’ll still be working on it on the side.

item.title: Thinking About the Next Project
item.description:
Posted on July 24, 2021

The next project…

Now that I’ve got most of the website running and working (the art side can wait for another time), I think it’s about time I get working on some actual projects. It’s been a while since I’ve used Unity so I think it’s about time I get refreshed and get started on a game. I probably won’t be able to update the blog until I get some significant progress done and have some images/gifs to show for it. So with that said, onto the planning phase!

item.title: Image Resizing and Soundcloud embeds
item.description:
Posted on July 21, 2021

Image resizing

I finally figured out how to resize images through Hugo shortcodes. I mean, just look at this L O N G boi. He seems to be trapped in some pretty strict margins, but it’s fine as it is. I can also make him a T A L L boi too. I can also position him properly:

This is the only image I have so far, but I’ll add some more later.

Soundcloud embeds

I also managed to get soundcloud embeds working through shortcodes as well. As a dark theme user, I don’t really like their white background. There’s probably a way to change it through CSS or HTML, but I haven’t found a solution yet.

For now, they’ll just look like this:

item.title: The beginning of the website
item.description:
Posted on July 19, 2021

Today I felt like making a website. I mean, I had always meant to do it at one point, but I had always put it off until today. Github Pages and Hugo made it seem like it would be pretty easy to make one, but boy was I wrong. I spent pretty much all day trying to get it to work, but at least it seems to work now, even if it does look pretty barebones right now. I still gotta learn a bunch of stuff to make this blog look a little better, so I’ll work on this website for now before diving into other things.

I also need to figure out how to format images like this on these sites too lmao
